methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
C12 Quantum Electronics is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Matthieu and Pierre Desjardins that has raised $19.4M to date. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis applies here, using the $19.4M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement. This method yields an implied valuation of $39M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while 7 same-sector quantum computing peers exist, fewer than 3 filtered comparables at the same stage or adjacent tier with similar valuation profiles are available to establish a reliable median.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $33M (low) to $45M (high), with a median of $39M. Key uncertainties include the sparsity of same-stage peer data in quantum computing—a nascent sector where comparable transaction multiples remain limited—and the absence of revenue or unit economics, which typically anchor later-stage valuations. Any refinement would require additional Series A quantum computing transactions or revenue-based metrics from C12.
